recent_decisions.title: 2026/27 Police Budget and Council Tax Precept Approved
recent_decisions.political_summary: The panel approved a £15 (5.4%) increase in the Band D council tax precept for policing, noting a £4.934m funding gap despite a £8.694m police grant increase. 66% of 2,026 respondents supported the tax rise.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Band D council tax precept increase for 2026/27

motions.text: That the Commissioner’s proposal to increase the council tax precept by £15 (5.4%) for a Band D property in 2026/27 be approved.

recent_decisions.committee: Children & Young People Overview and Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Approval of previous meeting minutes
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously approved the minutes from the last meeting held on 3rd November 2025.

motions.text: That the minutes of the last meeting held on 3rd November 2025 be agreed as a correct record and signed by the Chair.

recent_decisions.title: Police Finance Settlement and Investments Noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The panel noted the provisional police finance settlement, including £295.218m grant funding and investments in officer numbers and digital policing.

motions.text: That details of the 2026/27 provisional police finance settlement, cost pressures and investments and the impact on Lancashire’s budget be noted.

recent_decisions.committee: Children & Young People Overview and Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Request for detailed performance metrics on Children's Social Care Reforms
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee requested detailed performance metrics on the Children's Social Care Reforms to be provided at the next meeting.
key_data_points: Performance metrics to be provided at next meeting
Family Help model reduced escalations to child protection services by one third

motions.text: That the detailed performance metrics be provided at the next meeting

recent_decisions.title: Formal Response to Commissioner's Proposals
recent_decisions.political_summary: The panel agreed to submit a formal written response to the Commissioner's proposals by 8th February 2026.

motions.text: That arrangements be made to ensure that a formal written response to the proposals was sent to the Commissioner by 8th February 2026.

recent_decisions.title: Draft Capital Investment Programme Noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The panel noted the draft capital investment programme for 2026/27 to 2030/31, including delayed projects due to inflation.

motions.text: That the draft capital investment programme for the period 2026/27 to 2030/31 be noted.

recent_decisions.title: Commissioner's Reserves Position Noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The panel noted the Commissioner's reserves position, with £13.796m in General Reserves and £8.298m in Earmarked Reserves as of 31st March 2026.

motions.text: That the position of the Commissioner’s reserves be noted.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of previous meeting minutes
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the minutes of the previous meeting held on 23rd September 2025.

recent_decisions.title: Note external audit progress update
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the external audit progress update, which highlighted delays due to backstop arrangements.
key_data_points: Audit delayed due to backstop arrangements
Focus on key balance sheet items

recent_decisions.title: Note draft auditors annual report 2024/25
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the draft auditors annual report, which included a disclaimer opinion expected in February 2026.

recent_decisions.title: Note mid-year treasury management review 2025/26
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the mid-year treasury management review, confirming no changes to the strategy approved in March 2025.
key_data_points: No changes to treasury management strategy

recent_decisions.title: Note audit & assurance progress and outcomes
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the audit and assurance progress report, including discussions on fraud prevention measures.

recent_decisions.title: Approved Pay Policy Statement 2025-26
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council approved the Pay Policy Statement for 2025-26, outlining salary payments and pay scales for employees. No vote counts were recorded.
key_data_points: Includes Chief Executive and Chief Officers' pay scales

recent_decisions.title: Budget robustness and financial reserves noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council noted the robustness of the 2026/27 budget calculations and the adequacy of financial reserves, acknowledging a High Needs Budget deficit.
key_data_points: Government announcements on SEND reforms expected

recent_decisions.title: 2026/27 General Revenue Fund Budget approved
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council approved the 2026/27 General Revenue Fund Budget, with 32 voting for, 11 against, and 1 abstention. Key decisions included setting aside £2.317m for High Needs DSG deficits.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Funding for High Needs DSG deficits
key_data_points: £2.317m set aside for High Needs DSG
Budget deficit on High Needs DSG noted

recent_decisions.title: Approved Persimmon Homes' Bank Hey Farm development with £7.5m S106 agreement
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved Persimmon Homes' planning application for Bank Hey Farm, requiring a £7,556,401 S106 agreement for infrastructure and community benefits. The vote was unanimous.
recent_decisions.financial_context: S106 agreement for infrastructure and community benefits
key_data_points: Includes highway improvements and school places

motions.text: That subject to a Section 106 Agreement to secure payment of a commuted sum totalling £7,556,401...

recent_decisions.title: Approved minor material amendment for 16 Cranshaw Drive roof extension
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved a retrospective application for roof alterations at 16 Cranshaw Drive, removing obscure glazing conditions and allowing roof lights.

motions.text: That the application be approved subject to conditions set out in the Directors report.

recent_decisions.title: Approved shipping container storage at Queens Park
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved listed building consent for a shipping container storage unit opposite Old Bowling Hut in Queens Park.

motions.text: That the application be approved subject to the conditions highlighted in the Directors report.

recent_decisions.committee: Children & Young People Overview and Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Minutes of previous meeting approved
recent_decisions.political_summary: The minutes from the last meeting were agreed as a correct record and signed by the Chair.

motions.text: That the minutes of the last meeting held on 3rd November 2025 be agreed as a correct record and signed by the Chair.

recent_decisions.committee: Children & Young People Overview and Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: No declarations of interest received
recent_decisions.political_summary: No councillors declared any interests in the matters discussed.

motions.text: That the detailed performance metrics be provided at the next meeting.

recent_decisions.committee: Children & Young People Overview and Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Youth MPs update deferred to future meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Youth Forum update was postponed due to absence of representatives.

recent_decisions.committee: Children & Young People Overview and Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Progress on Ofsted recommendations noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee reviewed progress on Ofsted recommendations for children's services, including care leavers, reunification, homeless youth support, and supervision practices.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Costs discussed for residential care placements vs foster care
key_data_points: Monthly data sharing introduced for homeless youth

recent_decisions.title: Approval of minutes from previous meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The minutes from the previous meeting were approved with a correction noting Councillor Julie Gunn's attendance. No vote recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of 2025-2026 Schools Capital Programme
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Executive Board approved the 2025-2026 Schools Capital Programme, including budget increases for several projects due to unforeseen costs. The vote was unanimous.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Total additional funding approved for various school projects
key_data_points: £8,000 increase for Longshaw Infant School kitchen
£15,000 increase for Lower Darwen Primary School fire doors
£26,000 increase for Avondale School toilet refurbishment
£12,500 increase for Avondale School boiler replacement

motions.proposer: Executive Member for Children, Young People and Education
motions.text: That the attached list of projects, funded from the School Condition Allocation and Devolved Formula Capital, and as detailed in Appendix 2 attached to the report for inclusion in the 2025 - 2026 Schools and Education capital programme, be approved.

motions.proposer: Executive Member for Children, Young People and Education
motions.text: That the following variations to the 2024 - 2025 capital programme be approved: budget increases for Longshaw Infant School, Newfield Special School, Lower Darwen Primary School, Avondale School toilet refurbishment, and Avondale School boiler replacement.

motions.proposer: Executive Member for Children, Young People and Education
motions.text: That the Strategic Director of Children’s and Education be granted delegated authority in consultation with the Executive Member for Children, Young People and Education to undertake the procurement for the works in accordance with the Contracts and Procurement Procedure Rules in the Council’s Constitution.

motions.proposer: Executive Member for Children, Young People and Education
motions.text: That, Subject to (5) below, the expenditure to be incurred on individual projects, in line with the Council’s Financial Procedure Rules, be approved.

motions.proposer: Executive Member for Children, Young People and Education
motions.text: That it be agreed the projects proposed to be funded from s106 Agreements would not commence until funding under these agreements had been received in full by the Council.

motions.proposer: Executive Member for Children, Young People and Education
motions.text: That the reports provided for the Executive Member be noted, and that these reports would detail any variations/amendments to programmes of work and would seek the necessary approvals where required to ensure compliance.
